commit.message:
fix: migrate from iText 5 (AGPL) to OpenPDF 3.0.2 (LGPL) and fix SSRF in PDF rendering (#536)

* docs: add PDF dependency consolidation plan

Comprehensive plan to migrate from 5+ PDF libraries (iText 5 AGPL,
XMLWorker, ultrabuk, Flying Saucer, PDFBox) down to 2 (OpenPDF LGPL
+ PDFBox). Covers 45 Java files across 4 phases with risk assessment,
API migration mapping, and testing strategy.

Key decisions:
- Replace iText 5.5.13.5 (AGPL) with OpenPDF 2.0.x (LGPL)
- Keep PDFBox 2.0.35 for merge/split/encrypt operations
- Keep Flying Saucer 9.13.3 (already uses OpenPDF transitively)
- Defer Doc2PDF removal and ultrabuk removal to separate PRs
- No browser JS needed for PDF rendering (only PDF-embedded Acrobat JS)

https://claude.ai/code/session_01TRZBbahEcwdwf4v31NoTDK

* feat: migrate 45 files from iText 5 (AGPL) to OpenPDF 2.0.5 (LGPL)

Migrate all PDF creation code from com.itextpdf.* to com.lowagie.*
(OpenPDF 2.0.5), reducing license risk and consolidating PDF libraries.

Changes across 46 files:
- Add explicit OpenPDF 2.0.5 dependency (matches Flying Saucer 9.13.3)
- Replace all com.itextpdf.text.* imports with com.lowagie.text.*
- Replace BaseColor (iText-specific) with java.awt.Color (4 files)
- Replace Font.FontFamily enum with Font int constants (1 file)
- Replace iText codec.Base64 with java.util.Base64 (FaxImporter)
- Consolidate PdfWriterFactory: remove deprecated lowagie overloads,
  migrate active iText overload to OpenPDF with full functionality
- Update inline BadPasswordException reference (FaxImporter)

Intentionally deferred:
- Doc2PDF.java stays on iText (uses XMLWorkerHelper, separate PR)
- ultrabuk-htmltopdf-java kept as-is (separate PR)
- iText 5 dependency remains in pom.xml for Doc2PDF only

No functional changes to PDF output. All PDF templates (150+ AcroForm
PDFs), embedded Acrobat JavaScript (auto-print), and PDF manipulation
operations are unaffected by the import migration.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01TRZBbahEcwdwf4v31NoTDK

* fix: migrate test files from iText to OpenPDF imports

Migrate 2 test files missed in the initial bulk migration:
- LabPDFCreatorTest.java: com.itextpdf.text.DocumentException → com.lowagie.text
- FaxImporterCriticalGapsTest.java: all iText imports → OpenPDF equivalents,
  replace codec.Base64.encodeBytes() with java.util.Base64.getEncoder()

Signed-off-by: Michael Yingbull <michael@maplecreekmedical.ca>

* fix: detect TIFF by extension before Image.getInstance() in ImagePDFCreator

OpenPDF 3.0.x removed the built-in TiffImage codec, so Image.getInstance()
throws for TIFF files. The previous code called Image.getInstance() first and
the TIFF handler branch was unreachable for actual TIFFs.

Fix: check file extension to detect TIFFs before attempting Image.getInstance(),
routing TIFF files directly to the ImageIO/TwelveMonkeys handler. Also:
- Use a generic error message (no raw path) in the missing-reader exception
- Wrap reader.dispose() in a finally block to prevent resource leak

Co-authored-by: Michael Yingbull <yingbull@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: validate imagePath with PathValidationUtils in ImagePDFCreator

Validate imagePath against DOCUMENT_DIR using PathValidationUtils.validateExistingPath()
before any filesystem access in printPdf(). Centralizes validation once before the TIFF
and non-TIFF branches, preventing path traversal attacks. Also removes raw path from
the non-TIFF error log message to avoid exposing potentially sensitive file paths.

Co-authored-by: Michael Yingbull <yingbull@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: resolve review bot findings for OpenPDF migration PR

Addresses items #1-9 and #11-13 from the code review summary:

#1 PdfWriterFactory: replace multiple setPageEvent() calls with
   PdfPageEventForwarder to prevent stampers from silently overwriting
   each other (confidentiality/promo text was never stamping)

#2 PdfWriterFactory: throw IllegalStateException instead of returning
   null on DocumentException to fail fast and prevent NPEs in callers

#3 ImagePDFCreator: wrap all TIFF/non-TIFF processing in try/finally
   to guarantee document.close() runs even when exceptions are thrown

#4 FHIRCommunicationRequestHandler: use try-with-resources for PdfReader
   to prevent resource leak if getNumberOfPages() throws

#5 FrmPDFServlet: guard Integer.parseInt(cfgVal[5]) with try/catch
   NumberFormatException, falling back to font size 12 with a log warning

#6 pom.xml: declare openpdf-html 3.0.2 as an explicit direct dependency
   (LabPDFCreator directly imports HTMLWorker from this module)

#7 FaxImporter: switch Base64.getDecoder() to getMimeDecoder() to handle
   MIME-formatted (line-wrapped) Base64 payloads from fax providers;
   add explicit null check for missing document payload

#8 LabPDFCreator: remove unused MessageHandler handler parameter from
   private getTextColor() method and update its call site

#9 ReplacedElementFactoryImpl: update outdated comment that still
   referenced "old lowagie Image class" — now uses OpenPDF 3.x

#11 ImagePDFCreator: add null check for ImageInputStream returned by
    ImageIO.createImageInputStream() before passing to getImageReaders()

#12 docs/pdf-dependency-consolidation-plan.md: add blank lines around
    Group E and Group F tables to satisfy markdownlint MD058

#13 FHIRCommunicationRequestHandler: remove raw fileName from error log
    to avoid leaking file path information

Co-authored-by: Michael Yingbull <yingbull@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(upload): send multipart/form-data with 'chunk' field

Longhorn's backing-image-manager doUploadFromRequest calls
request.MultipartReader() which requires Content-Type: multipart/form-data.
The file data must be in a form field named 'chunk'.

- harvester-vm.sh: Python3 uploader now streams a multipart envelope:
  part header → file bytes → part footer, with Content-Length set to
  the total multipart body size.
- image/main.tf: curl fallback uses -F 'chunk=@file' instead of -T (which
  sent raw application/octet-stream).

The ?size=<bytes> query parameter (required by Longhorn) is retained.

commit.message:
Fix Lock 'STATE_ACCESS' wrongful returner warning in DoCheckin (#2864)

* Initial plan

* Fix Lock 'STATE_ACCESS' wrongful returner in DoCheckin

- DoCheckin was ignoring the return value of ObtainLock, always setting
  transStateOwner/transOwner to true even when the lock was not acquired.
  This caused ReleaseLock to be called when not the lock owner, triggering
  the "attempt to return by X -- but not owner!" warning.
- Use the ObtainLock return value to set transStateOwner/transOwner,
  consistent with DoRecoverMisfires and ExecuteInNonManagedTXLock.
- Fix ThreadLockKey.Equals to use string.Equals instead of ReferenceEquals
  to align with the value-based GetHashCode.
- Fix SimpleSemaphore.GetLock to use string.Equals for consistency.

Co-authored-by: lahma <171892+lahma@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Rewrite OBJ/MTL parser to byte-stream based with StreamReader, add mmap support, and integrate Windows long path/Unicode support (#416)

* Initial plan

* Add max_iter guards to parsing loops and mmap support for .obj/.mtl loading

Co-authored-by: syoyo <18676+syoyo@users.noreply.github.com>

* Rewrite parser from line-based to byte-stream-based with EOF guards

Replace safeGetline + raw const char* token pointer scanning with a
StreamReader class that wraps (buf, len, idx) with bounds-checked
peek/advance/match operations. Every byte access is guarded by an EOF
check.

Key changes:
- Add StreamReader class for bounds-checked text parsing
- Add sr_* parse functions that use StreamReader instead of const char**
- Add LoadMtlInternal, LoadObjInternal, LoadObjWithCallbackInternal
  using StreamReader; public APIs wrap these
- mmap paths construct StreamReader directly from mapped memory
- Remove safeGetline (no longer needed)
- Keep old parse functions for backward compat with public
  ParseTextureNameAndOption API
- Keep IS_SPACE, IS_NEW_LINE, IS_DIGIT macros for old parse functions

feat: Support URL Tools (#325)

Users couldn't consume HTTP-hosted tools that aren't distributed via
GitHub Releases. This adds a first-class `#URL` source type for
downloading tools from arbitrary HTTP/HTTPS endpoints.

## New: `#URL` source type

```cue
runtime: schema.#ToolsRuntime & {
    platforms: ["linux-x86_64", "darwin-arm64"]
    tools: mytool: {
        version: "1.2.3"
        overrides: [
            {os: "linux", arch: "x86_64", source: schema.#URL & {
                url: "https://example.com/releases/{version}/mytool-linux-amd64.tar.gz"
                path: "mytool"
            }},
            {os: "darwin", arch: "arm64", source: schema.#URL & {
                url: "https://example.com/releases/{version}/mytool-darwin-arm64.tar.gz"
                path: "mytool"
            }},
        ]
    }
}
```

Template variables `{version}`, `{os}` (darwin/linux), and `{arch}`
(aarch64/x86_64) are expanded at resolution time. Supports the same
extraction model as GitHub sources: tar.gz, zip, raw binary, and typed
`extract` rules (bin, lib, include, pkgconfig, file).

## Changes

- **`schema/tools.cue`** — Adds `#URL` with `url!`, `path?`, `extract?`
fields; extends `#Source` union
- **`crates/core`** — Adds `SourceConfig::Url` (manifest
deserialization) and `ToolSource::Url` (lockfile serialization)
- **`crates/tools/url/`** — New `cuenv-tools-url` crate implementing
`UrlToolProvider`; caches to
`~/.cache/cuenv/tools/url/<name>/<version>/`
- **`crates/cuenv`** — Registers `UrlToolProvider` in both
`create_registry()` call sites; handles `SourceConfig::Url` in
`source_config_to_tool_source()`

fix: fix `#meanwhile` being ignored inside grid cells, boxes, and other containers (#274)

* Initial plan

* Fix meanwhile not working in grids, boxes, and other container elements

When `#meanwhile` appears inside container elements (grid cells, boxes,
blocks, etc.), it was previously ignored. The outer context would hide
the container based on accumulated repetitions from preceding pauses,
without considering the `meanwhile` reset inside the container.

Fix by returning `final-repetitions` (the actual post-`meanwhile` value)
as a 4th element from `_parse-content-into-results-and-repetitions`.
All container-processing branches now propagate the `meanwhile` effect:
- Flush accumulated `hidden-parts` when `meanwhile` is detected
- Use `calc.min(repetitions, final-repetitions)` for show/hide decision
- Update `repetitions = final-repetitions` for subsequent items

Fixes: #259

commit.message:
Add Prettier formatting for markdown in pre-commit and CI (#11)

This pull request introduces improvements to both the code formatting
workflow and the documentation for architectural analysis and training
strategies. The most significant changes are the addition of Prettier
markdown checks to CI and pre-commit hooks, new Prettier configuration
and ignore files, and extensive enhancements to the
`resolution_and_architecture_analysis.md` report to clarify recommended
architectures, upsampling methods, and training strategies.

**Code Quality and Workflow Improvements:**

* Added Prettier markdown checks to CI workflow
(`.github/workflows/ci.yml`) and pre-commit hooks (`.husky/pre-commit`)
to enforce consistent formatting of markdown files.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-b803fcb7f17ed9235f1e5cb1fcd2f5d3b2838429d4368ae4c57ce4436577f03fR46-R56)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-d2bc4bbf14eadc292d84e0ef5f7a93115c23557f533809fc80b896934291529dR8)
* Introduced `.prettierrc` with prose wrapping configuration and
`.prettierignore` to exclude common directories from formatting checks.
[[1]](diffhunk://#diff-663ade211b3a1552162de21c4031fcd16be99407aae5ceecbb491a2efc43d5d2R1-R3)
[[2]](diffhunk://#diff-b640b344ee7f3f03d2a443795a5d0708ef50e2e6e34214109ab2aad13ad6ba98R1-R5)

**Documentation Enhancements:**

* Expanded
`docs/reports/20251123_resolution_and_architecture_analysis.md` with
detailed recommendations for optimal resolutions, deeper architectures,
upsampling strategies, attention layer guidelines, and phased
training/testing approaches.

feat: Israel Post priority tracking + performance overhaul

- Add israelpost.service.ts: direct Israel Post tracking via Playwright
  scraping of doar.israelpost.co.il (bypasses bot protection)
- Rewrite tracking strategy: Israel Post API first for Israeli packages,
  17track first for non-Israeli packages, with proper fallback chains

  (no more Canada/China locations for Israel Post packages)
- Add Israeli location validation (Hebrew chars or known city names)
- Speed up sync: batch size 5→10, delay 2s→500ms, parallel pools
  for Israel Post + 17track packages
- Fix Google Places: add region=il bias, reject all non-Israeli results
- Enhanced BAD_LOCATION_PATTERNS with foreign country/city names
- Location cleanup runs after every sync to scrub bad data

> *This section details on the original issue you should resolve*
> 
> <issue_title>Feature: Allow format-specific encode quality control via
query parameter</issue_title>
> <issue_description>### Background
> With the transition to `libvips` (v3.0), we want to provide users with
fine-grained control over the image encoding quality. However, optimal
quality values differ significantly between formats (e.g., WebP at
`q=80` might look good, but AVIF at `q=80` could result in unnecessarily
large files).
> 
> To solve this, we should provide a simple yet flexible query parameter
API that allows clients to specify quality either universally or
per-format.
> 
> ### Proposal
> Introduce a `q` (quality) query parameter that accepts both a single
integer for universal quality, or a comma-separated format-specific
string.
> 
> ### Query Parameter Specification
> The `q` parameter can take the following forms:
> 
> 1. **Universal Quality:**
>    `?q=80`
> Applies a quality of `80` to whatever format the image is eventually
encoded to (WebP, AVIF, JPEG, etc.).
> 
> 2. **Format-Specific Quality:**
>    `?q=avif:60&q=webp:80`
>    `?q=avif:60,webp:80`
> Applies `60` when the image is negotiated to be served as AVIF, and
`80` when served as WebP.
> If a format is not explicitly defined in the string, it should fall
back to a default value.
> 
> 3. **Combined:**
>    `?q=70&q=avif:50`
>    `?q=70,avif:50`
> Can optionally support a base quality with format overrides (applies
`50` to AVIF, and `70` to anything else).
> 
> ### Configuration & Defaults
> To maintain stability, the server should have configurable default
values when the `q` parameter is missing or invalid:
> 
> - `MANAEL_DEFAULT_QUALITY`: Default fallback quality (e.g., `80`).
> - If the parsed quality value is out of bounds (e.g., `< 1` or `>
100`), it should be clamped to the nearest valid value (`1` or `100`),
or fall back to the default.
> 
> ### Example Requests
> - `GET /image.jpg?q=80`
> - `GET /image.jpg?q=avif:50&q=webp:70`
> - `GET /image.jpg?q=avif:40,webp:75`</issue_description>

fix: pass ?size=<bytes> to Longhorn backing image upload endpoint

Harvester's upload action handler (backingimage/upload.go) proxies the
request to Longhorn Manager's backing image endpoint and passes through
req.URL.RawQuery. Longhorn requires a 'size' query parameter containing
the image size in bytes. Without it, Longhorn fails immediately with:

  upload failed: strconv.ParseInt: parsing "": invalid syntax

This caused the upload to 'succeed' structurally (HTTP connection stayed
open, nginx kept waiting) but Longhorn had already rejected it, resulting
in a 504 gateway timeout after the full body was sent.

Fix:
- harvester-vm.sh: append &size={bytes} to req_path in Python3 uploader
  (size is already computed via os.path.getsize for the progress display)
- image/main.tf: compute IMAGE_SIZE via wc -c and append &size= to URL

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(shared): prevent IP spoofing in rate limiter (#V7 H-17)

The rate limiter blindly trusted X-Forwarded-For and X-Real-IP headers,
allowing attackers to bypass per-IP rate limiting by forging those headers.

Changes:
- Add configureRateLimiter({ trustProxy }) opt-in (default: false)
- When trustProxy is false, all requests share one 'anonymous' bucket
  (secure by default — no spoofable header is trusted)
- When trustProxy is true, validate IP format via isValidIP() before
  using the leftmost X-Forwarded-For entry as client IP
- Log warnings on invalid IP formats in forwarded headers
- Export extractClientIp() for reuse by other rate-limiting code
- Update route-rate-limit.ts to use extractClientIp() instead of
  inline header parsing

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(workflow): expose POEDITOR_TOKEN to locale audit step (#8203)

The `Run locale audit` step lacked `POEDITOR_TOKEN` in its environment,
causing `npm run locale:audit` to always fail with `POEDITOR_TOKEN
environment variable is required`. Additionally, `audit-only` mode
skipped the token presence check despite the audit script requiring the
token to call the POEditor API.

## Changes

- **Job-level `env`**: Moved `POEDITOR_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.POEDITOR_TOKEN
}}` from the download step to the job level, making it available to all
steps including `Run locale audit`
- **Token check**: Removed `if: inputs.operation_mode != 'audit-only'`
guard from `Check POEditor Token` — the check is required for all modes
since the audit calls the POEditor API
- **Cleanup**: Removed now-redundant step-level `env` block from
`Download locales from POEditor`

```yaml
jobs:
  download:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      POEDITOR_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.POEDITOR_TOKEN }}   # ← was only on the download step
```

<!-- START COPILOT ORIGINAL PROMPT -->

<summary>Original prompt</summary>

> Fix failing GitHub Actions workflow job 66047598996 (Locale Download
POEditor) where `npm run locale:audit` fails with `POEDITOR_TOKEN
environment variable is required`.

> Context:
> - Workflow: `.github/workflows/locale-poeditor-download.yml`
> - Job logs show the workflow checks `${{ secrets.POEDITOR_TOKEN }}`
and prints configured, but later the locale audit script
(`locale/scripts/locale-audit.js`) fails because it reads
`process.env.POEDITOR_TOKEN` and the audit step does not export the
secret to the environment.
> - Download step already sets `env: POEDITOR_TOKEN: ${{
secrets.POEDITOR_TOKEN }}` but the audit step does not.
> - Additionally, audit-only mode currently skips the token check but
still runs the audit, which requires the token.

> Required changes:

or job-level `env`). Prefer a job-level `env` for clarity and to cover
all steps, but ensure it does not break the `audit-only` mode.
> 2) Ensure `Check POEditor Token` behavior aligns with audit-only mode:
> - Either run the token check for audit-only mode too, or otherwise
guarantee that audit-only runs won’t fail due to missing token.
> 3) Keep changes minimal and focused on fixing the failing job.

https://github.com/ChurchCRM/CRM/actions/runs/22769871384/job/66047598996

fix(shared): add value-level PII detection to sanitizePII

The PII sanitizer previously only redacted fields whose keys matched
sensitive patterns (e.g. 'password', 'token'). Fields with innocuous
keys but PII-containing values (e.g. { data: 'user@example.com' })
were passed through unsanitized.

Add redactPIIValues() which scans string values against regex patterns
for email addresses, SSNs, credit card numbers, and phone numbers,
replacing matches with [REDACTED]. Existing key-based checks remain
intact and take priority (full value replacement).

Resolves: H-20

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix OpenRouter base URL (https://api.openrouter.ai)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(shared): harden XSS sanitizer against comprehensive attack vectors

Replace the naive script-tag-only sanitizer with a multi-layered approach:
- Decode HTML entities (&#x, &#, named) before processing
- Strip null bytes and zero-width characters
- Remove 16 dangerous tag families (script, svg, math, iframe, etc.)
- Strip event handler attributes (on*=...)
- Remove dangerous URI schemes (javascript:, data:, vbscript:)
- Remove style-based XSS (expression(), url(), -moz-binding, behavior)
- Remove template literal injection (${...})
- HTML-encode all core dangerous characters (&, <, >, ", ', /)

Closes H-22

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix(shared): prevent SSRF via IP interpolation in ip-device-utils

- Add strict IPv4/IPv6 format validation (isValidIP, isValidIPv4, isValidIPv6)

  covering 10.x, 172.16-31.x, 192.168.x, 127.x, 169.254.x, 100.64-127.x,
  ::1, fc00::/7, fe80::/10, multicast, and more
- Validate IP format in getClientIP() before returning header values
- Use URL constructor + encodeURIComponent() for safe geolocation URL building
- Replace ad-hoc private IP checks with centralized isPrivateOrReservedIP()

Closes H-19

Harden safe_commit.sh: expand directory args to changed files only

When workflows pass 'state/' as a bare directory, the conflict
resolution path would restore ALL state files from our commit,
potentially overwriting concurrent changes to files we didn't touch.

Now: directory args are expanded to only the individual files that
actually changed before committing. The conflict resolution path
logs each file being restored for easier debugging.

Affects: process-inbox, zion-autonomy, heartbeat-audit workflows
(all pass state/ as argument).

fix: use /v1/harvester/ prefix for Harvester upload action URL

Harvester's custom Steve action handlers (including ?action=upload)
are registered on Harvester's OWN router at:
  /v1/harvester/{type}/{namespace}/{name}?action=upload
(see pkg/server/router.go in harvester/harvester)

The paths we were trying:
  /v1/{type}/...           -> Rancher's Steve (no Harvester custom actions)
  /k8s/clusters/local/v1/ -> Rancher cluster proxy (no custom formatters)

Both correctly return 403 'no such action upload'.

Fix:
- harvester-vm.sh: poll and upload via upload_base + /v1/harvester/...
  (actions.upload in the response will now be populated by Harvester's
  Formatter, so the URL extraction also works as the primary path)
- image/main.tf: update API_PATH to use v1/harvester/ prefix
  (SERVER is already the host-only URL from harvester_server local)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
